tests.name: platform_module_present
tests.details: FAIL: docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md missing. Source file is named 'mcp-server.md' (hyphen) but init.sh:1124 looks for '${PLATFORM}.md' where PLATFORM='mcp_server' (underscore). The cp never matches and the print_info 'No platform module' branch is taken silently. verify-install.sh subsequently warns 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing'.

tests.name: lint_uat_scenarios_helper_copied

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with '[FAIL] Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' (PR #18 confirmed)

tests.name: init_gov_mode_flag_split_observed
tests.details: Initial test attempt passed --gov-mode and value via shell variable expansion ($GOV unquoted with two whitespace-separated tokens) — init.sh parsed it as a single arg 'sponsored_poc' and rejected it as 'Unknown option: --gov-mode sponsored_poc'. After re-running with two literal flag tokens it parsed correctly. Likely cause: word-splitting on the value side after IFS handling in init.sh's getopt-style loop. Investigation deferred — possibly a documentation gap rather than a code bug; flagged below.

framework_bugs.summary: platform module mcp_server.md missing because file is named mcp-server.md
framework_bugs.expected: docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md (or mcp-server.md) present in generated project, matching whatever PLATFORM identifier verify-install.sh expects
framework_bugs.actual: docs/platform-modules/ is empty. Source at $FRAMEWORK/docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md (hyphen) does not match init.sh:1124 lookup '$SCRIPT_DIR/docs/platform-modules/${PLATFORM}.md' where PLATFORM=mcp_server (underscore). verify-install.sh expects mcp_server.md and warns. Either rename the source file to mcp_server.md or normalize PLATFORM with tr '_' '-' before the lookup.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh --help-non-interactive does not warn that passing combined flags via an unquoted shell variable (e.g. GOV='--gov-mode sponsored_poc'; ... $GOV) can fail. Direct invocation with two literal tokens works.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh help text (around line 2610-2625), and the runbook example at RUNBOOK.md:120-125 which uses GOV variable pattern

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh returned 0 with 'Phase gates consistent.'

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: After all 5 phase1 steps completed and check-phase-gate.sh passed, .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase still equal to 0. No script in the framework auto-advances current_phase. Test continued by manually setting .current_phase = 2.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all phase1_architecture steps, run check-phase-gate.sh successfully, then jq -r '.current_phase' .claude/phase-state.json
framework_bugs.expected: current_phase advances to 2 after the gate passes (or a documented manual step is required)
framework_bugs.actual: current_phase stays at 0 indefinitely. No --advance-phase or equivalent CLI in process-checklist.sh / check-phase-gate.sh.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1'

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 5 build_loop steps (tests_written through documentation_updated) succeeded sequentially after stub artifact creation

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' returned 0; commit a4bf156 created

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeded (rc=0, commit 01e9b9c). PreToolUse hooks do not fire for shell-direct git commands. Reproduces the still-open U-C from the prior sweep.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 + --complete-step build_loop:feature_recorded, started uat-feat-2, completed all 5 build_loop steps with stubs, committed 'feat(uat-2): stub feature 2' successfully (commit 514c57f). PR #17 BL-006 grace-window fix confirmed working.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema (question/options/recommendation/offered_at). Simulated PreToolUse with `echo '{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git commit -am test"}}' | bash scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh` → returned permissionDecision=deny with the rich pending-approval reason. PR #16 BL-015 sentinel reader confirmed working.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel; subsequent simulated PreToolUse no longer denies on BL-015 grounds

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .claude/build-progress.json features_completed = ['uat-feat-1'] (and uat-feat-2 after second record)

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: shell-direct git commit bypasses BL-006 commit-msg gate
framework_bugs.reproduction: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(some-trigger): no loop' (without --start-feature, without going through PreToolUse path)
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should refuse a feat: subject when no Build Loop has been recorded for it
framework_bugs.actual: Commit succeeds. The gate is implemented in pre-commit-gate.sh which only runs as a PreToolUse hook; shell-direct git is not intercepted. Confirms still-open U-C from 2026-04-25 sweep.

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-006 should be backed by a real git hook (commit-msg or pre-commit) installed by init.sh, not just a Claude PreToolUse hook. As written, BL-006 only protects the agent path, not direct human/script commits.

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: N/A under stub mode: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a populated <html> file with a JS scenarios array. The script is present and functional (rejects bad input cleanly with exit 2), but a meaningful lint pass requires a real UAT template generation step that this UAT pass did not exercise. Marked N/A.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched through gate_passed) completed sequentially with rc=0

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh returned 1 with multiple warnings: 'Phase 0→1: gate date not recorded', 'Phase 1→2: gate date not recorded', 'Phase 1→2 backstop: could not load host driver', 'Phase 1→2: PROJECT_BIBLE.md not found'. These warnings reflect that the UAT skipped artifact creation for those gates (acknowledged stub limitation), but the framework correctly flagged them. Functional behavior: PASS-by-design (gate refuses to advance without artifacts). The Phase 1→2 backstop 'could not load host driver' is more concerning — see bug below.

framework_bugs.summary: check-phase-gate.sh logs cryptic 'could not load host driver' when manifest host field absent
framework_bugs.reproduction: init with --git-host other, complete phase1, run check-phase-gate.sh
framework_bugs.expected: Either resolve host=other gracefully without warning, or print a more actionable message
framework_bugs.actual: Logs '[WARN] Phase 1→2 backstop: could not load host driver (manifest host field may be missing; run scripts/check-gate.sh --backfill-host)'. The remediation (--backfill-host) is plausible but unverified; suggestion is the only signal a user gets that this is benign.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: By design: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 returned 1 with '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in sponsored poc mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3.' This is the expected behavior for sponsored_poc and is the correct gating; not a bug. Marking the test PASS-by-design — the runbook's Section E does not apply to sponsored_poc base agents.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: N/A for sponsored_poc — Phase 4 entry blocked by gov-mode policy. Correct framework behavior.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ entries (the latter is where this report writes its result file). Framework HEAD unchanged: d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 == captured-at-start.

summary: Base sponsored_poc / mcp_server / standard run completed end-to-end. PR #16 (BL-015 sentinel), PR #17 (BL-006 grace window, U-H pending-approval/lint helpers copied), PR #18 (framework-self guard, fake-remote tolerance), PR #19 (init.sh --non-interactive) all confirmed working. One new Medium bug found: docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md (hyphen) does not match init.sh's PLATFORM=mcp_server (underscore) lookup, so the platform module never lands in the project. U-C and U-D from the prior sweep re-confirmed as still open.
